extracted.brandBackground: Kona Ice businesses provide flavored shaved ice, blended beverages, ice cream, and related products to the general public in a mobile environment.

leadership.name: Tony Lamb
leadership.role: Chief Executive Officer, President, Secretary and Board Member
leadership.background: Mr. Lamb has served as CEO, President, and Secretary since inception in February 2008.
leadership.whyItMatters: He is the founder and has led the brand since its inception.

leadership.name: Matthew Perelman
leadership.background: Co-Founder and Managing Partner of Garnett Station Partners since September 2013.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings private equity and retail investment experience to the board.

leadership.name: Rafael Haramati
leadership.background: Managing Director at Garnett Station Partners since January 2020.

leadership.name: Robert A. Whitehouse II
leadership.background: CEO of Eagle Financial Services, Inc. since August 2014.
leadership.whyItMatters: Connects the brand with its primary indirect financing partner.

item19.notes: The franchisor does not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance or the past financial performance of company-owned or franchised outlets.

lineItems.notes: Paid upon signing the Franchise Agreement.
Low estimate assumes buying a used KEV; high estimate is for a new KEV.
Covers travel, lodging, and food for additional attendees.
Delivery fee from manufacturer in Florence, Kentucky.
Fee to modify KEV to meet local health department requirements.
Estimated insurance cost for the first three months.

lineItems.category: Tax, Title, and Licensing of KEV
lineItems.notes: Includes sales tax of approximately 6% of the purchase price.
Includes mixing drum, sanitation kit, cups, flavorings, and uniforms.
Local permits and licenses required to operate.
Low estimate assumes home storage; high estimate is for outdoor storage.
Cost of computer and required software.
Working capital for the first three months of operation.

flatMonthlyFees.name: KonaOS Business Management Software Fee (per KEV)
Monitor Content Management Software Fee (per monitor)

hiddenCosts.name: Brand Fund Contribution
hiddenCosts.description: $1,000 per year for KEVs and KEV 2.0 Trucks, plus $200 per year for each piece of Additional Equipment.

hiddenCosts.name: Royalty
hiddenCosts.description: Flat annual fee of $3,000 for years 1-2, $4,000 for years 3-6, and $5,000 for years 7-10.

hiddenCosts.name: Smoothie Royalty
hiddenCosts.description: Additional $1,000 per year if the franchisee elects to offer smoothies.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: The franchise agreement requires you to resolve disputes with the franchisor by mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation only in Kentucky.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Your spouse must sign a document that makes your spouse liable for all financial obligations under the franchise agreement.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: You must make minimum royalty or advertising fund payments, regardless of your sales levels.

extracted.conceptRationale: Kona Ice is a mobile food truck concept serving shaved ice and blended beverages, which fits the Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) category.
extracted.staffingRationale: The business is operated out of a mobile truck (KEV) which typically requires only one or two operators at a time.

riskReasons: No Item 19 financial performance representation — earnings are undisclosed, which is itself a caution.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Royalty % not found; variable costs may be understated.
Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $171,666 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion.

insights.conceptRationale: Kona Ice is a mobile food truck concept serving shaved ice and blended beverages, which fits the Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) category.

benchmark.label: QSR / fast-casual
benchmark.dominantRisk: Prime cost and throughput per labor hour. Smaller footprint helps fixed costs, but COGS + labor still set the ceiling.
considerations: Budget COGS ~28–33% and labor ~22–28%; target prime cost under ~60%.
Verify average ticket and daily transaction counts with Item 20 operators, not the franchisor.
Watch for required remodels and equipment refresh cycles buried in Item 6/Item 11.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–18% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 11–17% — the 22–28% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “QSR / fast-casual” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Kona Ice Holdings, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
